name: mur

metadata.name: mur
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Mur CLI for publishing agents and tools to your own PyPI server.

documentation:
# mur

```bash
pip install mur 
```

This CLI allows you to manage your AI agents and tools in combindation with the [murmur](https://murmur-nexus.github.io/murmur/) package.

To get started quickly, follow our [Getting Started with the Examples](https://murmur-nexus.github.io/murmur/tutorial/getting-started-with-examples/) tutorial.

functions.name: mur.cli.main
documentation:
Mur CLI: Package, share, manage AI agents and tools.

A command-line interface for managing Mur artifacts, including creation,
building, installation, and publishing of AI agents and tools.

functions.name: mur.cli.logout
documentation:
Clear stored credentials and tokens.

Removes all stored authentication credentials and tokens from the system.

Args:
    verbose (bool): If True, enables detailed output logging.

Raises:
    click.Abort: If clearing credentials fails.

classes.name: mur.cli.MurCLI

methods.name: mur.cli.MurCLI.__call__
documentation:
Override the call method to handle exceptions.

Args:
    *args: Variable length argument list.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments.

Returns:
    The result of self.main(*args, **kwargs).

Raises:
    click.Abort: If any exception occurs during command execution.

Custom command group for better error handling.

This class extends click.Group to provide enhanced error handling for the Mur CLI.
It catches exceptions and logs them appropriately before raising click.Abort.

functions.name: mur.commands.new_artifact.new_command
documentation:
Create the new command for Click.

Creates and configures a Click command for generating new artifacts with
proper argument validation and handling.

Returns:
    click.Command: Configured Click command for creating new artifacts.

classes.name: mur.commands.new_artifact.NewArtifactCommand

methods.name: mur.commands.new_artifact.NewArtifactCommand.__init__
documentation:
Initialize new artifact command.

Args:
    artifact_type: Type of artifact to create ('agent' or 'tool').
    name: Optional name for the artifact.
    verbose: Whether to enable verbose output.

methods.name: mur.commands.new_artifact.NewArtifactCommand._configure_yaml
documentation:
Configure YAML parser settings.

Returns:
    YAML: Configured YAML parser with specific formatting settings.

methods.name: mur.commands.new_artifact.NewArtifactCommand._create_build_manifest
documentation:
Create the murmur-build.yaml file with template structure.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If the configuration file already exists or creation fails.

methods.name: mur.commands.new_artifact.NewArtifactCommand.execute
documentation:
Execute the new artifact command.

Creates a new artifact by generating the necessary configuration files.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If artifact creation fails.

Handles creation of new artifacts.

This class manages the creation of new agent or tool artifacts by generating
the necessary configuration files and directory structure.

functions.name: mur.commands.publish_artifact.publish_command
documentation:
Create the publish command for Click.

Returns:
    click.Command: Configured Click command for publishing artifacts to the Murmur registry.

classes.name: mur.commands.publish_artifact.PublishCommand

methods.name: mur.commands.publish_artifact.PublishCommand.__init__
documentation:
Initialize publish command.

Args:
    verbose (bool): Whether to enable verbose output. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If the artifact type in murmur.yaml is invalid.

methods.name: mur.commands.publish_artifact.PublishCommand._build_package
documentation:
Build the package for publishing.

Returns:
    tuple[Path, list[str]]: A tuple containing:
        - dist_directory (Path): Directory containing the built files
        - package_files (list[str]): List of built package filenames

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If the package build process fails.

methods.name: mur.commands.publish_artifact.PublishCommand._publish_files
documentation:
Publish built package files to registry.

Args:
    config (PackageConfig): Package configuration containing metadata
    dist_dir (Path): Directory containing built files
    package_files (list[str]): List of package files to publish

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If the publishing process fails
    CodeError: If there's an error during the publishing process

methods.name: mur.commands.publish_artifact.PublishCommand.execute
documentation:
Execute the publish command.

This method orchestrates the entire publishing process including:
1. Determining the appropriate registry
2. Building the package
3. Publishing the files

Raises:
    Exception: If any step of the publishing process fails

Handles artifact publishing operations.

This class manages the process of building and publishing artifacts to the Murmur registry.
Supports both agent and tool artifact types.

functions.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.install_command
documentation:
Create the install command for Click.

Creates a Click command that handles the installation of Murmur artifacts
from a murmur.yaml configuration file.

Returns:
    click.Command: Click command for installing artifacts

classes.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand.__init__
documentation:
Initialize install command.

Args:
    verbose: Whether to enable verbose output

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand._get_murmur_packages_dir
documentation:
Get the murmur packages directory path.

Args:
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact (e.g., 'agents', 'tools')

Returns:
    Path: Path to site-packages/murmur/<artifact_type>/

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand._install_package
documentation:
Install a package using pip with configured index URLs.

Args:
    package_name (str): Name of the package to install
    version (str): Version of the package to install ('latest' or specific version)
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact ('agents' or 'tools')

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If package installation fails

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand._check_murmur_installed
documentation:
Check if the main murmur package is installed.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If murmur package is not installed

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand._update_init_file
documentation:
Update __init__.py file with import statement.

Updates or creates the __init__.py file in the appropriate murmur package directory
with an import statement for the installed artifact.

Args:
    package_name (str): Name of the package to import
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact ('agents' or 'tools')

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand._install_artifact_group
documentation:
Install a group of artifacts of the same type.

Installs multiple artifacts and their dependencies. For agents, also installs
their associated tools.

Args:
    artifacts (list[dict]): List of artifacts to install from yaml config
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact ('agents' or 'tools')

methods.name: mur.commands.install_artifacts.InstallArtifactCommand.execute
documentation:
Execute the install command.

Reads the murmur.yaml configuration file from the current directory and
installs all specified agents and tools.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If installation fails

Handles artifact installation.

This class manages the installation of Murmur artifacts (agents and tools) from
a murmur.yaml configuration file.

functions.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.build_command
documentation:
Create the build command for Click.

Returns:
    Click command for building artifacts

classes.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand.__init__
documentation:
Initialize build command.

Args:
    verbose: Whether to enable verbose output

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._configure_yaml
documentation:
Configure YAML parser settings.

Configures a YAML parser with specific formatting settings for consistent
file generation and parsing.

Returns:
    YAML: Configured YAML parser with specific formatting settings.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._load_build_manifest
documentation:
Load manifest from murmur-build.yaml.

Returns:
    dict: Manifest configuration dictionary.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If manifest file is missing or invalid YAML.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._create_directory_structure
documentation:
Create the artifact directory structure.

Creates the necessary package directories and files for the artifact,
including the murmur namespace package structure and source files.
If source files exist in the current directory, they will be copied over.
If no source files exist, a default main.py will be created.

Args:
    artifact_path (Path): Root path for new artifact.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If directory creation fails, required files are missing,
        or source file copying fails.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._create_project_files
documentation:
Create all necessary project files.

Creates README.md and pyproject.toml files for the artifact with appropriate
content based on the build configuration.

Args:
    artifact_path (Path): Root path for new artifact.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If file creation fails.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_pyproject_toml
documentation:
Generate pyproject.toml content.

Combines all project configuration sections into a complete pyproject.toml file.

Returns:
    str: Complete content for pyproject.toml file.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_build_system
documentation:
Generate build-system section.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the build-system section of pyproject.toml.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_project_section
documentation:
Generate project section including metadata.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the project section of pyproject.toml, including
        name, version, description, and other metadata.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_authors
documentation:
Generate authors section if author info exists.

Creates the authors section of pyproject.toml based on provided metadata.

Args:
    metadata (dict): Dictionary containing author metadata including 'author'
        and optional 'email' fields.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the authors section of pyproject.toml.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_classifiers
documentation:
Generate classifiers section.

Args:
    license_type: Type of license for the project, if any.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the classifiers section of pyproject.toml.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_dependencies
documentation:
Generate dependencies section.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the dependencies section of pyproject.toml.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_project_urls
documentation:
Generate project.urls section.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the project.urls section of pyproject.toml.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._generate_build_targets
documentation:
Generate build targets section.

Returns:
    list[str]: Lines for the build targets section of pyproject.toml.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand._write_filtered_build_manifest
documentation:
Filter and write configuration to murmur-build.yaml.

Writes a filtered version of the configuration to the artifact's
murmur-build.yaml file, including only the relevant keys for the
artifact type. For agents, this includes the 'instructions' key.

Args:
    artifact_path (Path): Path to new artifact.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If writing config fails.

methods.name: mur.commands.build_artifact.BuildCommand.execute
documentation:
Execute the build command.

Creates a new artifact project with the specified configuration,
including directory structure, project files, and filtered config.
If the artifact directory already exists, the build will be skipped.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If build process fails at any stage.

Handles artifact building.

This class manages the process of building Murmur artifacts (agents or tools)
by creating the necessary directory structure and configuration files.

Attributes:
    verbose (bool): Whether to enable verbose output.
    current_dir (Path): The current working directory.
    yaml (YAML): Configured YAML parser instance.
    build_manifest (dict): The loaded build manifest configuration.
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact ('agent' or 'tool').
    config (dict): The build configuration (alias for build_manifest).

classes.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand.__init__
documentation:
Initialize artifact command.

Args:
    command_name: Name of the command
    verbose: Whether to enable verbose output

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand._get_index_urls_from_murmurrc
documentation:
Get index URLs from .murmurrc file.

Args:
    murmurrc_path: Path to .murmurrc file.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple containing:
        - str: The primary index URL
        - list[str]: List of extra index URLs

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If .murmurrc file does not exist.
    ValueError: If index-url is not found in config.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand._ensure_murmurrc_exists
documentation:
Create or update .murmurrc with index URLs from environment or defaults.

Creates a new .murmurrc file if it doesn't exist, or updates the existing one.
Uses environment variables MURMUR_INDEX_URL and MURMUR_EXTRA_INDEXES if available,
otherwise falls back to default values.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand._ensure_murmur_namespace_in_path
documentation:
Ensure murmur packages directory is in Python path.

Adds the .murmur_packages directory from the user's home directory to sys.path
if it's not already present.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand.get_current_dir
documentation:
Get current working directory.

Returns:
    Path to current directory

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If current directory cannot be accessed

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand.handle_error
documentation:
Handle command errors consistently.

Args:
    error: The exception that occurred.
    message: Error message prefix to display before the error details.

Raises:
    click.Abort: Always raised after logging the error.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand.log_success
documentation:
Log success message in green color.

Args:
    message: Success message to display.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand._configure_yaml
documentation:
Configure YAML parser settings.

Returns:
    YAML: Configured YAML parser instance with specific formatting settings.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand._load_murmur_yaml_from_current_dir
documentation:
Load installation manifest from murmur.yaml in current directory.

Returns:
    PackageConfig: Package configuration from the installation manifest.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If murmur.yaml is not found or cannot be loaded.

methods.name: mur.commands.base.ArtifactCommand._load_murmur_yaml_from_package
documentation:
Load build manifest from murmur-build.yaml in package entry folder.

Searches for murmur-build.yaml in both agent and tool package directories.

Returns:
    PackageConfig: Package configuration from the build manifest.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If murmur-build.yaml is not found in any package directory.

Base class for artifact-related commands.

This class provides common functionality for commands that interact with artifacts,
including configuration management, authentication, and registry operations.

Attributes:
    REGISTRY_COMMANDS: List of commands that require registry configuration.

classes.name: mur.core.cache.CacheError
documentation: Raised when credential caching operations fail.

classes.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.__init__
documentation:
Initialize credential cache.

The cache uses 'mur' as the service name for keyring operations.

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.save_access_token
documentation:
Save access token securely.

Args:
    access_token: The access token string to store.

Raises:
    CacheError: If saving the access token fails.

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.load_access_token
documentation:
Load access token from secure storage.

Returns:
    The stored access token if found, None otherwise.

Raises:
    CacheError: If loading the access token fails.

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.clear_access_token
documentation:
Clear stored access token.

Raises:
    CacheError: If clearing the access token fails.

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.save_password
documentation:
Save password securely.

Args:
    password: The password string to store.

Raises:
    CacheError: If saving the password fails.

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.load_password
documentation:
Load password from secure storage.

Returns:
    The stored password if found, None otherwise.

Raises:
    CacheError: If loading the password fails.

methods.name: mur.core.cache.CredentialCache.clear_password
documentation:
Clear stored password.

Raises:
    CacheError: If clearing the password fails.

Handles secure storage and retrieval of credentials.

This class provides methods to securely store and retrieve access tokens
and passwords using the system keyring.

functions.name: mur.commands.uninstall_artifacts.uninstall_command
documentation:
Create the uninstall command.

Returns:
    click.Command: A Click command for package uninstallation.

classes.name: mur.commands.uninstall_artifacts.UninstallArtifactCommand

methods.name: mur.commands.uninstall_artifacts.UninstallArtifactCommand._uninstall_package
documentation:
Uninstall a package using pip.

Args:
    package_name (str): Name of the package to uninstall.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If package uninstallation fails.

methods.name: mur.commands.uninstall_artifacts.UninstallArtifactCommand._remove_from_init_file
documentation:
Remove package import from __init__.py if it exists.

Args:
    package_name (str): Name of the package whose import should be removed.
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact ('agents' or 'tools').

Note:
    Silently continues if the package or init file cannot be found.

methods.name: mur.commands.uninstall_artifacts.UninstallArtifactCommand.execute
documentation:
Execute the uninstall command.

Raises:
    click.ClickException: If the uninstallation process fails.

Handles package uninstallation.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the package to uninstall.
    verbose (bool): Whether to enable verbose logging output.

classes.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationError
documentation:
Raised when authentication operations fail.

This exception is used throughout the authentication module to indicate
failures in authentication-related operations such as login attempts,
credential management, and token validation.

classes.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager.__init__
documentation:
Initialize authentication manager.

Args:
    config_manager: Configuration manager instance
    base_url: Base URL for the registry API
    verbose: Whether to enable verbose output

Raises:
    AuthenticationError: If initialization fails

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager.authenticate
documentation:
Get a valid access token, using cached credentials when possible.

This method implements the following authentication flow:
1. Try to use cached access token
2. Try to authenticate with cached credentials
3. Prompt user for credentials if needed

Returns:
    str: Valid access token for API authentication

Raises:
    AuthenticationError: If authentication fails at any step

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager._validate_token
documentation:
Validate if the token is still valid.

Args:
    token (str): Access token to validate

Returns:
    bool: True if token is valid, False otherwise

Note:
    Currently assumes token is valid if it exists.
    Should be updated to validate against server.

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager._authenticate
documentation:
Authenticate with username and password.

Attempts to authenticate against the server using provided credentials.

Args:
    username (str): Username for authentication
    password (str): Password for authentication

Returns:
    str | None: Access token if authentication successful, None otherwise

Note:
    On successful authentication, credentials are automatically cached.

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager._save_credentials
documentation:
Save credentials for future use.

Args:
    username: Username to save
    password: Password to save
    access_token: Access token to save

Raises:
    AuthenticationError: If credentials cannot be saved

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager._prompt_and_authenticate
documentation:
Prompt for credentials and authenticate.

Interactively prompts the user for credentials and attempts authentication.
Uses cached username if available.

Returns:
    str: Valid access token

Raises:
    AuthenticationError: If authentication fails
    click.Abort: If user cancels authentication

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager.clear_credentials
documentation:
Clear all stored credentials.

Removes all cached credentials including:
- Access token
- Password
- Username from configuration

Raises:
    AuthenticationError: If credentials cannot be cleared

methods.name: mur.core.auth.AuthenticationManager.create
documentation:
Create an AuthenticationManager with dependencies.

Factory method to create a new instance with proper configuration.

Args:
    verbose (bool, optional): Enable verbose logging. Defaults to False.
    base_url (str, optional): Base URL for the registry API.
        Defaults to MURMUR_SERVER_URL.

Returns:
    AuthenticationManager: Configured instance

Raises:
    AuthenticationError: If manager creation fails

Centralized authentication management for Mur CLI.

This class handles all authentication-related operations including token management,
credential caching, and user authentication flows.

Attributes:
    verbose (bool): Flag for enabling verbose logging output
    cache (CredentialCache): Instance for managing cached credentials
    config_manager (ConfigManager): Instance for managing configuration
    config (dict): Current configuration settings
    base_url (str): Base URL for the registry API

functions.name: mur.adapters.adapter_factory.get_registry_adapter
documentation:
Get the appropriate registry adapter based on environment.

Determines whether to use a public or private registry adapter based on
the MURMUR_INDEX_URL environment variable.

Args:
    verbose (bool, optional): Whether to enable verbose logging. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    RegistryAdapter: Registry adapter instance:
        - PrivateRegistryAdapter: If MURMUR_INDEX_URL is set
        - PublicRegistryAdapter: If MURMUR_INDEX_URL is not set

classes.name: mur.core.config.ConfigError
documentation:
Raised when configuration operations fail.

This exception is used to indicate errors during configuration file operations
such as saving or loading configuration data.

classes.name: mur.core.config.ConfigManager

methods.name: mur.core.config.ConfigManager.__init__
documentation:
Initialize configuration manager.

Args:
    config_file (Path | str): Path to configuration file. Defaults to CONFIG_FILE.

methods.name: mur.core.config.ConfigManager._load_config
documentation:
Load configuration from file.

Updates default configuration with values from file.
Silently uses defaults if file doesn't exist or is invalid.

Note:
    If the configuration file cannot be read or parsed, warning messages
    will be displayed and default values will be used.

methods.name: mur.core.config.ConfigManager.save_config
documentation:
Save current configuration to file.

Creates configuration directory if it doesn't exist.

Raises:
    ConfigError: If the configuration cannot be saved due to file system
        errors or permission issues.

methods.name: mur.core.config.ConfigManager.get_config
documentation:
Get current configuration.

Returns:
    ConfigDict: A copy of the current configuration dictionary to prevent
        direct modification of internal state.

Manages Mur CLI configuration.

This class handles loading, saving, and accessing configuration settings
for the Mur CLI application.

Attributes:
    config_file (Path): Path to the configuration file.
    config (ConfigDict): Dictionary containing configuration settings.

variables.name: mur.core.packaging.REQUIRED_MANIFEST_FIELDS
documentation: Basic fields for installation manifest

variables.name: mur.core.packaging.REQUIRED_BUILD_FIELDS
documentation: Keep existing build manifest requirements

classes.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageMetadata
documentation:
Package metadata from murmur.yaml.

Args:
    name: Package name
    version: Package version string
    description: Package description
    metadata: Additional package metadata dictionary
    dependencies: Optional list of package dependencies

classes.name: mur.core.packaging.ConfigurationError
documentation:
Raised when configuration validation fails.

This exception is raised when package configuration validation fails,
such as missing required fields or invalid YAML format.

classes.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig.__init__
documentation:
Initialize package configuration.

Args:
    config_path: Path to murmur.yaml configuration file
    is_build_manifest: Whether this is a build manifest (murmur-build.yaml)
                     or an installation manifest (murmur.yaml)

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig.name
documentation: str: The package name from configuration.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig.version
documentation: str: The package version from configuration.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig.description
documentation: str: The package description from configuration.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig.metadata
documentation: dict: Additional package metadata from configuration.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig.type
documentation: Get the artifact type from configuration data.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig._create_metadata
documentation: Create package metadata from configuration data.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig._is_tool
documentation:
Check if the configuration is for a tool.

Args:
    config: Optional configuration dictionary. If None, loads from file.

Returns:
    bool: True if configuration is for a tool package.

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageConfig._load_and_validate
documentation: Load and validate the murmur configuration file.

Handles package configuration loading and validation.

This class manages loading and validating murmur package configuration files,
supporting both build manifests (murmur-build.yaml) and installation manifests
(murmur.yaml).

classes.name: mur.core.packaging.BuildError
documentation:
Raised when package building fails.

This exception is raised for build-related errors such as missing dependencies,
invalid project structure, or build command failures.

classes.name: mur.core.packaging.BuildResult
documentation:
Results from a package build operation.

Args:
    dist_dir: Path to the distribution directory containing built packages
    package_files: List of built package filenames
    build_output: String output from the build process

classes.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageBuilder

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Initialize package builder.

Args:
    project_dir: Directory containing the Python project
    verbose: Whether to output verbose logging

Raises:
    BuildError: If project directory doesn't exist

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageBuilder._validate_project_structure
documentation:
Validate the project structure before building.

Raises:
    BuildError: If the project structure is invalid

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageBuilder.build
documentation:
Build the package.

Args:
    artifact_type: Type of artifact to build

Returns:
    BuildResult: Results from the build operation

Raises:
    BuildError: If the build process fails

methods.name: mur.core.packaging.PackageBuilder._get_build_artifacts
documentation:
Get the built package files from the dist directory.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple containing:
        - Path: The dist directory path
        - list[str]: List of package filenames

Raises:
    BuildError: If no build artifacts are found or dist directory is missing

Handles Python package building operations.

This class manages the building of Python packages, including validation
of project structure and execution of build commands.

classes.name: mur.adapters.private_adapter.PrivateRegistryAdapter

methods.name: mur.adapters.private_adapter.PrivateRegistryAdapter.publish_artifact
documentation:
Publish an artifact to the private PyPI registry.

Args:
    artifact_type (str): Type of the artifact being published.
    name (str): Name of the artifact.
    version (str): Version of the artifact.
    description (str): Description of the artifact.
    metadata (dict[str, Any]): Additional metadata for the artifact.
    file_path (Path | str): Path to the artifact file to publish.

Returns:
    dict[str, Any]: Response containing status and message about the publish operation.

Raises:
    CodeError: If artifact file is not found (201) or if publishing fails (200).

methods.name: mur.adapters.private_adapter.PrivateRegistryAdapter.get_package_indexes
documentation:
Get package indexes, prioritizing environment variables over .murmurrc config.

The method first checks environment variables (MURMUR_INDEX_URL and MURMUR_EXTRAS_INDEX_URL)
for package index URLs. If not found, falls back to reading from .murmurrc configuration file.

Returns:
    list[str]: List of package index URLs with primary index first.

Raises:
    CodeError: If no private registry URL is configured (807) or if reading configuration fails.

Adapter for private PyPI registry instances.

This adapter handles publishing artifacts to and retrieving package indexes from
private PyPI registries.

Args:
    verbose (bool, optional): Enable verbose logging output. Defaults to False.

classes.name: mur.adapters.base_adapter.RegistryAdapter

methods.name: mur.adapters.base_adapter.RegistryAdapter.publish_artifact
documentation:
Publish an artifact to the registry.

Args:
    artifact_type (str): Type of artifact being published
    name (str): Name of the artifact
    version (str): Version string of the artifact
    description (str): Description of the artifact
    metadata (dict[str, Any]): Additional metadata for the artifact
    file_path (Path | str): Path to the artifact file to publish

Returns:
    dict[str, Any]: Response data from the registry after publishing

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: This is an abstract method that must be implemented

methods.name: mur.adapters.base_adapter.RegistryAdapter.get_package_indexes
documentation:
Get list of package index URLs for installation.

Returns:
    list[str]: List of PyPI-compatible index URLs in priority order

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: This is an abstract method that must be implemented

Base adapter for registry interactions.

This abstract class defines the interface for registry adapters that handle
artifact publishing and package index management.

Args:
    verbose (bool, optional): Enable verbose logging output. Defaults to False.

documentation: Constants used throughout the Mur CLI.

variables.name: mur.utils.constants.PYPI_USERNAME
documentation: local defaults

variables.name: mur.utils.constants.PYPI_PASSWORD
documentation: local defaults

classes.name: mur.utils.error_handler.CodeError

methods.name: mur.utils.error_handler.CodeError.handle
documentation: Prints the error and exits the program with the appropriate code.

Custom exception class for handling application error codes.

Maps numeric error codes to human-readable messages across different categories:
- 0xx: Success
- 1xx: CLI Input/Usage
- 2xx: Local Filesystem
- 3xx: Local Package State
- 5xx: Authentication/Authorization
- 6xx: Remote Package Resolution
- 8xx: Network Operations

classes.name: mur.adapters.public_adapter.PublicRegistryAdapter

methods.name: mur.adapters.public_adapter.PublicRegistryAdapter.get_headers
documentation:
Get authentication headers for API requests.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: Headers dictionary containing Bearer token.

Raises:
    CodeError: If authentication fails.

methods.name: mur.adapters.public_adapter.PublicRegistryAdapter.publish_artifact
documentation:
Publish an artifact to the registry.

Args:
    artifact_type (str): Type of the artifact.
    name (str): Name of the artifact.
    version (str): Version of the artifact.
    description (str): Description of the artifact.
    metadata (dict[str, Any]): Additional metadata for the artifact.
    file_path (Path | str): Path to the artifact file.

Returns:
    dict[str, Any]: Server response containing artifact details.

Raises:
    CodeError: If file not found, connection fails, or server returns an error.

methods.name: mur.adapters.public_adapter.PublicRegistryAdapter._handle_error_response
documentation:
Handle error responses from the server.

Args:
    response (requests.Response): Response object from the server.

Raises:
    CodeError: With appropriate error code and message based on the response.

methods.name: mur.adapters.public_adapter.PublicRegistryAdapter.get_package_indexes
documentation:
Get package indexes from .murmurrc configuration.

Reads the primary index URL and any additional index URLs from the .murmurrc
configuration file. Falls back to the default index if configuration cannot be read.

Returns:
    list[str]: List of package index URLs with primary index first.

Adapter for the public Murmur registry.

This class handles interactions with the public Murmur registry, including authentication,
artifact publishing, and package index management.

Args:
    verbose (bool, optional): Enable verbose logging. Defaults to False.
